turn_methodology.scope: turn-focused freezing-of-gait risk heuristics from monocular pose
direct_literature_support: turning is a common trigger of freezing of gait
freezing of gait involves ineffective stepping
short shuffling steps and trembling are clinically relevant descriptors
gait initiation and gait modulation are especially impaired in freezers
heuristic_components: shuffle-like stepping proxy from pose velocity

references.title: Freezing of gait: moving forward on a mysterious clinical phenomenon
Gait Initiation Impairment in Patients with Parkinson's Disease and Freezing of Gait
Abnormalities of the First Three Steps of Gait Initiation in Patients with Parkinson's Disease with Freezing of Gait
Turning and unilateral cueing in Parkinson's disease patients with and without freezing of gait

limitations: not a validated clinical diagnostic model
depends on monocular pose quality and camera viewpoint
turn scores are literature-informed heuristics rather than validated coefficients
medical interpretation requires validation on labeled turning-FOG datasets

overall.confidence: Strong evidence of PD-like gait patterns
